explanation: The property expresses that, globally, whenever the autopilot is currently in the MANEUVER state, and both the pilot is in control (standby) and sensor data is good, this should cause (in the next state) an entry into the STANDBY state. This is captured by using the LTL template: 'G ((bool_exp1 & bool_exp2) -> (X bool_exp3))', where 'bool_exp1' is 'state_is_MANEUVER', 'bool_exp2' is '(standby & good)', and 'bool_exp3' is 'state_is_STANDBY'. This encodes both the correct trigger conditions and transition requirement described in the natural language specification.

This LTL property captures the requirement that whenever the system is in the MANEUVER state (state_is_MANEUVER), and both the pilot is in control (standby is true) and sensor data is good (good is true), then in the next moment the system should be in the STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY). This directly translates the requirement that the autopilot should change from MANEUVER to STANDBY when these two conditions are met.

The property describes that whenever the autopilot is currently in the MANEUVER state (represented by state_is_MANEUVER), and the pilot is in control (standby is true) and sensor data is good (good is true), the autopilot shall transition to the STANDBY state in the next step. LTL template 11 captures this causality: globally (G), if the antecedent (state_is_MANEUVER & standby & good) holds, then in the next step (X), state_is_STANDBY must hold. This approach ensures the desired state change whenever the specified conditions are true, closely mirroring the requirements in the natural language statement.

This LTL property asserts that whenever the autopilot is currently in the MANEUVER state (state_is_MANEUVER is true), and the pilot is in control (standby is true) and sensor data is good (good is true), then in the next step the state should be STANDBY (state_is_STANDBY is true). This captures the requirement that the autopilot must transition from MANEUVER to STANDBY under the specified conditions.

This LTL property expresses that whenever the autopilot is in the MANEUVER state and both the pilot is in control ('standby') and the sensor data is good ('good'), then, in the next step, the autopilot must enter the STANDBY state ('state_is_STANDBY'). This directly captures the requirement that the state change from MANEUVER to STANDBY must occur under those input conditions, by enforcing the transition in the next timestep after the conditions are satisfied.

This LTL property expresses that whenever the system is currently in the MANEUVER state, and at the next time step the pilot takes control (standby = True) and the sensor data is good (good = True), then in the next step the system should transition to the STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY = True). This captures the requirement that the autopilot should change its state from MANEUVER to STANDBY when both conditions (pilot control and good sensor data) hold. This assumes that being in MANEUVER is represented by state_is_MANEUVER = True.

This LTL property states that it is always the case that if the system is in the MANEUVER state and the pilot takes control (standby is true) and the sensor data is good, then in the next step, the autopilot will be in the STANDBY state. This directly captures the requirement that the autopilot must change from MANEUVER to STANDBY when those specific conditions are met.

This LTL property specifies that whenever the conditions 'pilot is in control (standby)' (standby is True) and 'sensor data is good' (good is True) are both satisfied while the autopilot is in the MANEUVER state (state_is_MANEUVER is True), then in the next step, the autopilot transitions to the STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY is True). This captures the requirement that the autopilot must change its state from MANEUVER to STANDBY under these conditions.

The requirement specifies that the system must ensure a state change (from MANEUVER to STANDBY) occurs when the pilot is in control (standby is true) and sensor data is good (good is true). The formula thus expresses that whenever the autopilot state is MANEUVER and both standby and good are true, in the next step the state should be STANDBY. This precisely encodes the expected transition with the required preconditions.

This LTL property expresses that whenever the autopilot is in the MANEUVER state (state_is_MANEUVER), the pilot is in control (standby), and sensor data is good (good), then in the next step, the autopilot will enter the STANDBY state (state_is_STANDBY). This captures the natural language requirement that the system must switch from MANEUVER to STANDBY when those conditions are met.
